title: Coronilla lassenii
taxonomy: Coronilla lassenii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Faboideae. The genus Coronilla is a group of legumes that are native to Europe and Asia. Coronilla lassenii is specifically native to the western United States. It is a small annual herb with yellow flowers that blooms in the late spring.
uses: Coronilla lassenii is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Coronilla lassenii has been traditionally used by the Native Americans for its medicinal properties, including treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems contain saponins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Coronilla lassenii has also been used as a treatment for sore throats and digestive issues. However, further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects as a medicinal plant.

intro: Coronilla lassenii, also known as Lassen's clover or Lassen's crown-vetch, is a perennial legume native to the Sierra Nevada mountain range in California. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and boost the immune system. Bioactive constituents of Coronilla lassenii include isoflavones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coronilla lassenii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or topical ointment to support overall health and well-being.
